subject: Statistics
title: Positive Predictive Value
description: Learn Positive Predictive Value in one minute.

hook.text: Your test is positive. The real question isn't how good the test is — it's how likely you are to actually be sick.

buildup.text: Positive predictive value is the probability of being truly sick given a positive test result.

discovery.text: PPV depends heavily on disease prevalence. A great test for a rare disease still gives mostly false positives.

twist.text: A test with 99% sensitivity and 95% specificity has a PPV of just 17% if the disease affects 1 in 100.

climax.text: PPV is what patients actually need to know. Sensitivity and specificity describe the test. PPV describes your situation.

punchline.text: A positive test doesn't mean you're sick. Check the PPV.

quiz.question: Why can a highly sensitive test still have a low positive predictive value?
options: If the disease is rare, false positives vastly outnumber true positives
PPV doesn't depend on disease prevalence
